mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in INDPRO signals improving production, operating leverage, and near-term earnings revisions; the always-on signal maps higher industrial production momentum to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes INDPRO at 99.0446 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in 50% or fewer walk-forward instances, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk; when unemployment momentum crosses higher, equity investors reduce expected earnings growth and the S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes UNRATE at 7.6 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered return is not below the unconditional average at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS is rising, inflation pressure and tighter discount rates jointly compress equity valuations; this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses above its 12-month change and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes CPIAUCSL at 232.77 and FEDFUNDS at 0.14
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase trigger has a nonnegative mean 60-day forward return or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals that households have both income and willingness to spend; the joint demand-labor impulse raises expected revenues and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change is positive and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes RSAFS at 418284 and PAYEMS at 135195
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with improving household demand
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS and PAYEMS signal fails to exceed the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 mean return by at least 25 basis points in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and future pressure on construction, credit, and durable-goods activity; when housing starts break lower, the S&P 500 earns negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes HOUST at 917 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST breakdowns are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if downside hit rate is indistinguishable from random.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum decelerates while FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, lower inflation risk supports real income and valuation multiples; this disinflation-easing interaction predicts positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses below its 12-month change and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
failure_condition: Reject if the disinflation plus non-rising FEDFUNDS trigger produces 20-day returns below the unconditional mean or if the estimated interaction coefficient is not positive out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS momentum signals broad job creation and rising wage income, which supports consumption and earnings expectations; when payroll growth crosses a high threshold, the S&P 500 posts positive 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change crosses above +200 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes PAYEMS at 135195 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-strength triggers do not produce a positive average 5-day S&P 500 return or if hit rate for positive returns is below 52% in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Negative GDPC1 growth signals broad contraction in real demand and lower aggregate earnings power; when real GDP growth turns negative, equity risk premia rise and the S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses below 0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot includes GDPC1 at 13665.4 as of t-6mo
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered mean return is not below baseline.
